explicit_text: My name is Kiyoshi Noji, born in 1899 in a village north of Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. I was raised in a farming family with samurai ancestry and became deeply involved in Christian ministry, attending Tohoku Gakuin University and later studying theology in the United States at the Pacific School of Religion and Princeton University. My journey to America in 1926 was inspired by my cousin Rev. Kikuchi, and I pursued my calling as a Christian minister despite early hardships and cultural adjustments. I have fond memories of my education and the Christian community that supported me both in Japan and the U.S.
